2606.17799.title: Position: Coding Benchmarks Are Misaligned with Agentic Software Engineering
2606.17799.paper_summary: Coding agents have become a major mode of software engineering, but the benchmarks we use to compare them were designed in a pre-agent era: they collapse model, harness, and environment into a single end-to-end score, typically computed against one reference solution, with no component-level signal for iteration. We argue that current coding benchmarks are misaligned with agentic software engineering. A coding agent in practice is not a model: it is a system harness -- a composite of models, harnesses, contexts, environments, and feedback signals, any one of which can move the benchmark score by margins comparable to those between adjacent model generations. We discuss three symptoms: (i) benchmark scores conflate the model with the rest of the harness; (ii) grading against a single reference solution penalises equally valid alternatives; and (iii) the absence of signal at the level of individual harness components makes the end-to-end system score difficult to iterate on.
2606.17799.paper_summary_zh: 編碼代理已成為軟體工程的一種主要模式，但我們用來比較它們的基準是在代理時代之前設計的：它們將模型、工具和環境合併為一個單一的端到端得分，通常是針對一個參考解決方案計算的，並且沒有針對迭代的組件級信號。我們認為當前的編碼基準與代理軟體工程不相符。實際上的編碼代理不是一個模型：它是一個系統工具——由模型、工具、上下文、環境和反饋信號的組合，任何一個都可以使基準得分變化，幅度可與相鄰模型世代之間的變化相當。我們討論了三個症狀：(i) 基準得分將模型與其餘工具混為一談；(ii) 針對單一參考解決方案的評分對同樣有效的替代方案進行懲罰；以及 (iii) 在個別工具組件層面缺乏信號使得端到端系統得分難以進行迭代。
2606.17799.author: Maria I. Gorinova et.al.
2606.17799.authors: Maria I. Gorinova, Macey Baker, Amy Heineike, Maksim Shaposhnikov, Rob Willoughby, Dru Knox
